heading: Supportive services
label: 12338 Supportive services
notes: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1990 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 1990section 1001(a) of Pub. L. 101–501section 8621 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1990 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 1990section 1001(a) of Pub. L. 101–501section 8621 of this title

children.heading: Authority
children.label: a Authority
children.text: The Commissioner shall carry out a program for making grants to a State, that has designated an independent State body under and provided for coordinated services under , for distribution by the chief executive officer under a State plan approved under to demonstrate successful program approaches to fill service gaps identified through State planning and advocacy efforts for any of the areas specified in paragraph (2).section 12336 of this titlesection 12337 of this titlesection 12337 of this title

that are designed to facilitate the provision of comprehensive community based services that are efficient, coordinated, and readily available through such activities as case planning, case management, intake and assessment, and information and referral; and

children.label: A
children.text: provide adequate and safe physical shelter to young individuals and the families of such individuals, especially in emergency circumstances;

children.label: B
children.text: provide transitional living services to young individuals who are homeless;

children.label: C
children.text: enable young individuals to attain and maintain physical and mental well-being;

children.label: D
children.text: provide health screening to detect or prevent illnesses, or both, that occur most frequently in young individuals as well as better treatment and counseling;

children.label: E
children.text: enhance the development of children to ensure that such children enter school prepared and ready to learn;

children.label: F
children.text: promote the highest quality of educational opportunity, especially through drop-out prevention programs, remediation for young individuals who have dropped out of school, and vocational education;

children.label: G
children.text: provide effective training apprenticeships and employment opportunities;

children.label: H
children.text: promote participation in community service and civic, cultural, and recreational activities that value young individuals as resources and promote self-esteem and a stake in the community;

children.label: I
children.text: promote the participation of young individuals in decisions concerning planning and managing the lives of such individuals;

children.label: J
children.text: encourage young individuals and the families of such individuals to use any community facilities and services that are available to such individuals;

children.label: K
children.text: ensure that young individuals who are unable to live with the biological families of such individuals have a safe place to live until such individuals can return home or move into independent adult life; and

children.label: L
children.text: prevent the abuse, neglect, or exploitation of young individuals.
